name: ovadare

metadata.name: ovadare
metadata.summary: A framework for conflict detection and resolution in multi-agent systems.

documentation:
![Logo of OVADARE](./docs/ovadare_logo.png) # **OVADARE** 🤖 **OVADARE**: Cutting-edge framework for detecting, classifying, and resolving conflicts between AI agents autonomously. Designed to integrate seamlessly with AutoGen, CrewAI and other AI orchestration platforms, OVADARE empowers multi-agent systems to handle complex tasks collaboratively and efficiently.

[Homepage](https://www.ovadare.com/) | [Documentation](https://docs.ovadare.com/) | [Examples](https://github.com/ovadare/ovadare-examples)

[![GitHub Repo stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/ovadare/ovadare)](https://github.com/ovadare/ovadare) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
## Table of contents - [Why OVADARE?](#why-ovadare) - [Getting Started](#getting-started) - [Key Features](#key-features) - [Examples](#examples) - [Conflict Detection](#conflict-detection) - [Policy Adjustment](#policy-adjustment) - [Resolution Automation](#resolution-automation) - [Integration with Autogen](#integration-with-autogen) - [Contribution](#contribution) - [License](#license) ## Why OVADARE? In a world where multi-agent systems are becoming the backbone of AI-driven solutions, OVADARE stands out by providing robust conflict detection, classification, and resolution capabilities. Whether it's resolving task overlaps, prioritization issues, or behavioral conflicts among agents, OVADARE ensures seamless collaboration within your AI ecosystem. ## Getting Started To get started with OVADARE, follow these simple steps: ### 1. Installation Ensure you have Python >=3.8 installed on your system. OVADARE can be installed via pip: '' pip install ovadare '' ### 2. Setting Up OVADARE To integrate OVADARE into your project, create an OVADARE conflict resolution manager: '' from ovadare.conflicts import ConflictDetector, ConflictResolver detector = ConflictDetector() resolver = ConflictResolver() # Detect conflicts conflicts = detector.detect_conflicts(agent_data) # Resolve conflicts resolved = resolver.resolve_conflicts(conflicts) '' ### 3. Configuring Policies OVADARE allows you to define custom policies for conflict resolution: '' from ovadare.policies import PolicyManager, Policy policy_manager = PolicyManager() custom_policy = Policy( name="TaskPrioritization", rules=[ "If two agents are assigned the same task, prioritize based on expertise.", "Resolve resource allocation conflicts using weighted scoring." ] ) policy_manager.add_policy(custom_policy) '' ### 4. Running with Autogen OVADARE integrates seamlessly with Autogen for multi-agent orchestration. Define agents and tasks using Autogen and let OVADARE handle the conflicts: '' from autogen import Agent, Task agents = [ Agent(name="Agent1", role="Planner"), Agent(name="Agent2", role="Executor") ] tasks = [ Task(name="Plan Project", assigned_to="Agent1"), Task(name="Execute Project", assigned_to="Agent2"), ] # Detect and resolve conflicts before execution conflicts = detector.detect_conflicts(tasks) resolved_tasks = resolver.resolve_conflicts(conflicts) '' ## Key Features - **Comprehensive Conflict Handling**: Detect, classify, and resolve agent-level conflicts. - **Customizable Policies**: Define dynamic policies tailored to your specific needs. - **Seamless Integration**: Works out-of-the-box with platforms like Autogen. - **Extensibility**: Easily extend functionality with custom rules and modules. - **Advanced Analytics**: Monitor and visualize conflict trends and resolution effectiveness. ## Examples ### Conflict Detection Detect conflicts in a multi-agent system with ease: '' conflicts = detector.detect_conflicts(agent_tasks) print(conflicts) '' ### Policy Adjustment Dynamically adjust policies based on feedback loops: '' policy_manager.adjust_policy("TaskPrioritization", new_rules=["If deadlines conflict, prioritize by urgency."]) '' ### Resolution Automation Automate resolution using AI-powered decision-making engines: '' resolved = resolver.resolve_conflicts(conflicts, method="ai-assisted") '' ## Integration with Autogen OVADARE enhances Autogen by adding robust conflict resolution capabilities to its agent orchestration framework. By integrating OVADARE, you can ensure that your agents collaborate effectively without stepping on each other's toes. ## Contribution OVADARE is open-source, and we welcome contributions. To contribute: 1. Fork the repository. 2. Create a new branch for your feature. 3. Add your feature or improvement. 4. Send a pull request. ### Running Tests '' pytest tests/ '' ### Static Analysis '' mypy ovadare/ '' ### Packaging '' python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel '' ## License OVADARE is released under the [MIT License](https://github.com/ovadare/ovadare/blob/main/LICENSE).

modules.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service
documentation:
Monitoring Service Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the MonitoringService class, which collects and reports
metrics and logs for the Ovadare framework. It helps in monitoring system health,
performance, and detecting potential issues.
ovadare/monitoring/monitoring_service.py

classes.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService

methods.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the MonitoringService.

Args:
    agent_registry (AgentRegistry): An instance of AgentRegistry.
    conflict_detector (ConflictDetector): An instance of ConflictDetector.
    interval (int): The interval in seconds at which metrics are collected.

methods.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService.start
documentation: Starts the monitoring service in a separate thread.

methods.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService.stop
documentation: Stops the monitoring service.

methods.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService._run
documentation: The main loop that collects and reports metrics at specified intervals.

methods.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService.collect_metrics
documentation: Collects and logs metrics from various components.

methods.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService.get_active_agents_count
documentation:
Retrieves the number of active agents.

Returns:
    int: The count of active agents.

methods.name: ovadare.monitoring.monitoring_service.MonitoringService.get_conflicts_detected_count
documentation:
Retrieves the number of conflicts currently detected.

Returns:
    int: The count of detected conflicts.
The MonitoringService collects and reports metrics and logs for the Ovadare framework.

modules.name: ovadare.feedback.feedback_manager
documentation:
Feedback Manager Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the FeedbackManager class, which handles the collection,
storage, and processing of feedback from agents and users. The feedback can
be used to improve policies, resolutions, and overall system performance.
ovadare/feedback/feedback_manager.py

classes.name: ovadare.feedback.feedback_manager.FeedbackManager

methods.name: ovadare.feedback.feedback_manager.FeedbackManager.__init__
documentation: Initializes the FeedbackManager.

methods.name: ovadare.feedback.feedback_manager.FeedbackManager.submit_feedback
documentation:
Submits feedback to the FeedbackManager.

Args:
    feedback_data (Dict[str, Any]): A dictionary containing feedback details.
        Expected keys include 'agent_id', 'user_id', 'feedback_type',
        'message', and 'timestamp'.

methods.name: ovadare.feedback.feedback_manager.FeedbackManager.get_all_feedback
documentation:
Retrieves all submitted feedback.

Returns:
    List[Dict[str, Any]]: A list of all feedback entries.

methods.name: ovadare.feedback.feedback_manager.FeedbackManager.get_feedback_by_agent
documentation:
Retrieves feedback submitted by a specific agent.

Args:
    agent_id (str): The ID of the agent.

Returns:
    List[Dict[str, Any]]: A list of feedback entries from the specified agent.

methods.name: ovadare.feedback.feedback_manager.FeedbackManager.process_feedback
documentation:
Processes collected feedback to identify trends or issues.
This method can be expanded to include analytics or integration
with policy updates.
Handles the collection, storage, and processing of feedback from agents and users.

modules.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager
documentation:
Policy Manager Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the PolicyManager class, which manages the loading, storage,
and evaluation of policies within the framework. It integrates with the
DynamicPolicyLoader to dynamically load policies from configured sources.
ovadare/policies/policy_manager.py

classes.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.__init__
documentation: Initializes the PolicyManager.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.load_policies
documentation: Loads policies using the DynamicPolicyLoader from the configured source.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.get_policies
documentation:
Retrieves the list of loaded policies.

Returns:
    List[Policy]: A list of Policy instances.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.get_policy_by_id
documentation:
Retrieves a policy by its ID.

Args:
    policy_id (str): The unique identifier of the policy.

Returns:
    Optional[Policy]: The Policy instance if found, else None.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.evaluate_policies
documentation:
Evaluates the action against all loaded policies.

Args:
    action (Dict[str, Any]): The action to evaluate.

Returns:
    List[EvaluationResult]: A list of evaluation results for each policy.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.add_policy
documentation:
Adds a new policy to the manager.

Args:
    policy (Policy): The policy to add.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.remove_policy
documentation:
Removes a policy by its ID.

Args:
    policy_id (str): The unique identifier of the policy to remove.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy_manager.PolicyManager.reload_policies
documentation: Reloads policies from the configured source.

The PolicyManager manages the loading, storage, and evaluation of policies.
It integrates with the DynamicPolicyLoader to load policies from configured sources.

modules.name: ovadare.policies.policy
documentation:
Policy Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the abstract Policy class and concrete implementations for defining
and evaluating policies within the framework. It includes the PolicyRule class for
defining individual rules that make up a policy.
ovadare/policies/policy.py

classes.name: ovadare.policies.policy.EvaluationResult
documentation: Represents the result of evaluating a policy or rule.

classes.name: ovadare.policies.policy.PolicyPriority
documentation: Enum-like class for policy priorities.

classes.name: ovadare.policies.policy.PolicyRule

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy.PolicyRule.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a PolicyRule.

Args:
    rule_id (str): The unique identifier of the rule.
    condition (Callable[[dict], bool]): A function that evaluates the rule condition.
    message (str): The message to return if the rule is violated.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy.PolicyRule.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluates the rule against an action.

Args:
    action (dict): The action to evaluate.

Returns:
    EvaluationResult: The result of the evaluation.
Represents a single rule within a policy.

classes.name: ovadare.policies.policy.Policy
documentation: Abstract base class for policies.

classes.name: ovadare.policies.policy.ConcretePolicy

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy.ConcretePolicy.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a ConcretePolicy.

Args:
    policy_id (str): The unique identifier of the policy.
    name (str): The name of the policy.
    description (str): A description of the policy.
    priority (str): The priority level of the policy.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy.ConcretePolicy.add_rule
documentation:
Adds a rule to the policy.

Args:
    rule (PolicyRule): The rule to add.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.policy.ConcretePolicy.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluates the action against all rules in the policy.

Args:
    action (dict): The action to evaluate.

Returns:
    EvaluationResult: The overall result of the policy evaluation.
A concrete implementation of a policy consisting of multiple rules.

modules.name: ovadare.policies.dynamic_policy_loader
documentation:
Dynamic Policy Loader Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the DynamicPolicyLoader class, which is responsible for
loading policies from various sources, such as JSON files, databases, or APIs.
ovadare/policies/dynamic_policy_loader.py

classes.name: ovadare.policies.dynamic_policy_loader.DynamicPolicyLoader

methods.name: ovadare.policies.dynamic_policy_loader.DynamicPolicyLoader.__init__
documentation: Initializes the DynamicPolicyLoader.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.dynamic_policy_loader.DynamicPolicyLoader.load_policies
documentation:
Loads policies from the specified source.

Args:
    source (Optional[str]): The source from which to load policies.
        If None, a default source is used (e.g., 'policies.json').

Returns:
    List[Policy]: A list of loaded Policy instances.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.dynamic_policy_loader.DynamicPolicyLoader._parse_policy
documentation:
Parses a dictionary representing a policy into a Policy instance.

Args:
    policy_dict (dict): The dictionary containing policy data.

Returns:
    Policy: An instance of a Policy.

Raises:
    ValueError: If required fields are missing or invalid.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.dynamic_policy_loader.DynamicPolicyLoader._parse_rule
documentation:
Parses a dictionary representing a policy rule into a PolicyRule instance.

Args:
    rule_dict (dict): The dictionary containing rule data.

Returns:
    PolicyRule: An instance of a PolicyRule.

Raises:
    ValueError: If required fields are missing or invalid.

methods.name: ovadare.policies.dynamic_policy_loader.DynamicPolicyLoader._compile_condition
documentation:
Compiles a condition code string into a callable function.

Args:
    condition_code (str): The code representing the condition.
    rule_id (str): The ID of the rule, used for logging.

Returns:
    Callable: A function that evaluates the condition.

Raises:
    Exception: If the condition code cannot be compiled.
The DynamicPolicyLoader is responsible for loading policies from a specified source.

modules.name: ovadare.agents.agent
documentation: ovadare/agents/agent.py

modules.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk
documentation:
Agent SDK Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the AgentSDK class, which allows agents to interact with
the Ovadare Framework. It supports authentication, authorization, action submission,
and feedback submission functionalities.
ovadare/agents/agent_sdk.py

classes.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk.AgentSDK

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk.AgentSDK.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the AgentSDK.

Args:
    api_base_url (str): The base URL for the API endpoints.
    authentication_manager (AuthenticationManager): Manages authentication.
    authorization_manager (AuthorizationManager): Manages authorization.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk.AgentSDK.register
documentation:
Registers the agent with the framework.

Args:
    agent_id (str): The unique ID of the agent.
    password (str): The password for the agent.

Returns:
    bool: True if registration is successful, False otherwise.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk.AgentSDK.login
documentation:
Authenticates the agent and obtains an authentication token.

Args:
    agent_id (str): The unique ID of the agent.
    password (str): The password for the agent.

Returns:
    bool: True if login is successful, False otherwise.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk.AgentSDK.submit_action
documentation:
Submits an action to the framework.

Args:
    action (Dict[str, Any]): The action data.

Returns:
    bool: True if submission is successful, False otherwise.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk.AgentSDK.submit_feedback
documentation:
Submits feedback to the framework.

Args:
    feedback_type (str): The type of feedback.
    message (str): The feedback message.

Returns:
    bool: True if submission is successful, False otherwise.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_sdk.AgentSDK.logout
documentation: Logs out the agent by clearing the authentication token.
Provides methods for agents to interact with the Ovadare Framework.

modules.name: ovadare.communication.api_endpoints
documentation:
API Endpoints Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the APIEndpoints class, which defines RESTful API endpoints
for agents and users to interact with the framework. It integrates authentication
and authorization mechanisms to ensure secure access to the framework's resources.
ovadare/communication/api_endpoints.py

classes.name: ovadare.communication.api_endpoints.APIEndpoints

methods.name: ovadare.communication.api_endpoints.APIEndpoints.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the APIEndpoints.

Args:
    agent_registry (AgentRegistry): The registry for managing agents.
    event_dispatcher (EventDispatcher): The event dispatcher for sending events.
    authentication_manager (AuthenticationManager): The authentication manager for securing endpoints.
    authorization_manager (AuthorizationManager): The authorization manager for access control.

methods.name: ovadare.communication.api_endpoints.APIEndpoints._register_routes
documentation: Registers the API routes.

methods.name: ovadare.communication.api_endpoints.APIEndpoints._get_user_id_from_token
documentation:
Retrieves the user ID from the authentication token.

Args:
    token (Optional[str]): The authentication token from the request header.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The user ID if the token is valid, else None.

methods.name: ovadare.communication.api_endpoints.APIEndpoints.start
documentation:
Starts the API server in a separate thread.

Args:
    host (str): The host IP address.
    port (int): The port number.

methods.name: ovadare.communication.api_endpoints.APIEndpoints.stop
documentation: Stops the API server.
Defines the API endpoints for the Ovadare Framework.

modules.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface
documentation:
Agent Interface Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the AgentInterface, which defines the methods that agents must
implement to interact with the Ovadare framework. Agents represent entities that perform
actions within the system and may need to respond to resolutions for conflicts.
ovadare/agents/agent_interface.py

classes.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.agent_id
documentation:
Returns the unique identifier of the agent.

Returns:
    str: The agent's unique ID.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.capabilities
documentation:
Returns the capabilities of the agent.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: A dictionary representing the agent's capabilities.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.initialize
documentation: Initializes the agent. Called when the agent is registered with the framework.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.shutdown
documentation: Shuts down the agent. Called when the agent is unregistered from the framework.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.perform_action
documentation:
Performs an action.

Args:
    action (Dict[str, Any]): A dictionary representing the action to perform.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: The result of the action.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.report_status
documentation:
Reports the current status of the agent.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: A dictionary representing the agent's status.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.handle_event
documentation:
Handles an event dispatched by the framework.

Args:
    event_type (str): The type of event.
    event_data (Dict[str, Any]): The data associated with the event.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_interface.AgentInterface.handle_resolution
documentation:
Handles a resolution provided by the ResolutionEngine in response to a conflict.

Args:
    resolution (Resolution): The resolution to apply.

Abstract base class for agents in the Ovadare framework.
Agents must implement this interface to interact with the framework.

modules.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry
documentation:
Agent Registry Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the AgentRegistry class, which is responsible for managing
the registration, retrieval, and lifecycle of agents within the framework.
ovadare/agents/agent_registry.py

classes.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry.AgentRegistry

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry.AgentRegistry.__new__
documentation: Implements the Singleton pattern to ensure only one instance of AgentRegistry exists.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry.AgentRegistry.register_agent
documentation:
Registers an agent with the registry.

Args:
    agent (AgentInterface): The agent to register.

Raises:
    ValueError: If an agent with the same ID is already registered.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry.AgentRegistry.unregister_agent
documentation:
Unregisters an agent from the registry.

Args:
    agent_id (str): The ID of the agent to unregister.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the agent is not found in the registry.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry.AgentRegistry.get_agent
documentation:
Retrieves an agent by its ID.

Args:
    agent_id (str): The ID of the agent to retrieve.

Returns:
    Optional[AgentInterface]: The agent if found, else None.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry.AgentRegistry.get_all_agents
documentation:
Retrieves a list of all registered agents.

Returns:
    List[AgentInterface]: A list of all registered agents.

methods.name: ovadare.agents.agent_registry.AgentRegistry.find_agents_by_capability
documentation:
Finds agents that have a specific capability.

Args:
    capability (str): The capability to search for.

Returns:
    List[AgentInterface]: A list of agents that have the specified capability.

The AgentRegistry manages the agents registered within the Ovadare framework.
It allows for registering new agents, retrieving existing agents, and unregistering agents.

modules.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict
documentation:
Conflict Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the Conflict class, which represents a detected conflict
resulting from a policy violation. It includes details about the violation,
the action that caused it, and the agent involved.
ovadare/conflicts/conflict.py

classes.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict.Conflict

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict.Conflict.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a Conflict instance.

Args:
    conflict_id (str, optional): The unique identifier of the conflict.
        If None, a UUID is generated.
    related_agent_id (str): The ID of the agent involved in the conflict.
    action (Dict[str, Any]): The action that caused the conflict.
    violation_details (str): Details about the policy violation.
    policy_id (str): The ID of the policy that was violated.
    timestamp (float, optional): The time the conflict was detected.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict.Conflict.to_dict
documentation:
Serializes the Conflict instance to a dictionary.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: A dictionary representation of the conflict.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict.Conflict._current_timestamp
documentation:
Gets the current timestamp.

Returns:
    float: The current time in seconds since the epoch.
Represents a detected conflict resulting from a policy violation.

modules.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine
documentation:
Resolution Engine Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the ResolutionEngine class, which is responsible for
generating resolutions for detected conflicts and communicating them to
the appropriate agents.
ovadare/conflicts/resolution_engine.py

functions.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.load_resolutions
documentation: Loads resolutions from persistent storage.

classes.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.ResolutionEngine

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.ResolutionEngine.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the ResolutionEngine.

Args:
    agent_registry (Optional[AgentRegistry]): An instance of AgentRegistry.
        If None, a new AgentRegistry is created.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.ResolutionEngine.generate_resolutions
documentation:
Generates resolutions for a list of conflicts.

Args:
    conflicts (List[Conflict]): A list of conflicts to resolve.

Returns:
    List[Resolution]: A list of generated resolutions.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.ResolutionEngine._create_resolution
documentation:
Creates a resolution for a single conflict.

Args:
    conflict (Conflict): The conflict to resolve.

Returns:
    Resolution: The generated resolution.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.ResolutionEngine.apply_resolutions
documentation:
Applies a list of resolutions by communicating them to the appropriate agents.

Args:
    resolutions (List[Resolution]): The resolutions to apply.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.ResolutionEngine._get_conflict_by_id
documentation:
Retrieves a conflict by its ID.

Args:
    conflict_id (str): The unique identifier of the conflict.

Returns:
    Optional[Conflict]: The Conflict instance if found, else None.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution_engine.ResolutionEngine._send_resolution_to_agent
documentation:
Sends a resolution to an agent.

Args:
    agent (AgentInterface): The agent to send the resolution to.
    resolution (Resolution): The resolution to send.

The ResolutionEngine generates resolutions for detected conflicts and
communicates them to the appropriate agents.

modules.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution
documentation:
Resolution Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the Resolution class, which represents a proposed resolution
for a detected conflict. It includes details about the corrective action to be taken,
the conflict it addresses, and an explanation.
ovadare/conflicts/resolution.py

classes.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution.Resolution

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution.Resolution.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a Resolution instance.

Args:
    resolution_id (Optional[str]): The unique identifier of the resolution.
        If None, a UUID is generated.
    conflict_id (str): The ID of the conflict this resolution addresses.
    corrective_action (Optional[Dict[str, Any]]): The action proposed to resolve the conflict.
    explanation (str): A description or explanation of the resolution.
    timestamp (Optional[float]): The time the resolution was generated.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution.Resolution.to_dict
documentation:
Serializes the Resolution instance to a dictionary.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: A dictionary representation of the resolution.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution.Resolution.from_dict
documentation:
Creates a Resolution instance from a dictionary.

Args:
    data (Dict[str, Any]): A dictionary containing resolution data.

Returns:
    Resolution: A new Resolution instance.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution.Resolution.__repr__
documentation:
Returns a string representation of the Resolution instance.

Returns:
    str: A string representation of the resolution.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.resolution.Resolution._current_timestamp
documentation:
Gets the current timestamp.

Returns:
    float: The current time in seconds since the epoch.
Represents a proposed resolution for a detected conflict.

modules.name: ovadare.utils.secrets_manager
documentation:
Secrets Manager Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the SecretsManager class, which securely manages sensitive
information like API keys, passwords, and encryption keys. It uses environment
variables and, optionally, encrypted files to store secrets securely.
ovadare/utils/secrets_manager.py

classes.name: ovadare.utils.secrets_manager.SecretsManager

methods.name: ovadare.utils.secrets_manager.SecretsManager.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the SecretsManager.

Args:
    encryption_key (Optional[bytes]): The key used for encryption and decryption.
        If None, it attempts to load from the 'SECRETS_ENCRYPTION_KEY' environment variable.

methods.name: ovadare.utils.secrets_manager.SecretsManager.get_secret
documentation:
Retrieves a secret value by its key.

Args:
    key (str): The key of the secret.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The decrypted secret value, or None if not found.

methods.name: ovadare.utils.secrets_manager.SecretsManager.set_secret
documentation:
Stores a secret value securely.

Args:
    key (str): The key for the secret.
    value (str): The secret value to store.

methods.name: ovadare.utils.secrets_manager.SecretsManager.generate_encryption_key
documentation:
Generates a new encryption key.

Returns:
    bytes: The generated encryption key.
Manages secrets securely using environment variables and encryption.

modules.name: ovadare.utils.configuration
documentation:
Configuration Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the Configuration class, which manages configuration
settings for the framework. It supports loading configurations from environment
variables, JSON files, and default settings.
ovadare/utils/configuration.py

classes.name: ovadare.utils.configuration.Configuration

methods.name: ovadare.utils.configuration.Configuration.load
documentation:
Loads configuration settings from a JSON file, environment variables,
and merges them with default settings.

Args:
    config_file_path (Optional[str]): Path to the configuration file.
        If None, uses the default path specified in the default configuration.

methods.name: ovadare.utils.configuration.Configuration.get
documentation:
Retrieves a configuration value by key.

Args:
    key (str): The configuration key.
    default (Any): The default value if the key is not found.

Returns:
    Any: The configuration value.

methods.name: ovadare.utils.configuration.Configuration.set
documentation:
Sets a configuration value.

Args:
    key (str): The configuration key.
    value (Any): The value to set.

methods.name: ovadare.utils.configuration.Configuration.get_all
documentation:
Retrieves all configuration settings.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: All configuration settings.
Manages configuration settings for the Ovadare Framework.

modules.name: ovadare.utils.logging_config
documentation:
Logging Configuration Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module configures logging settings for the framework, including log
formats, levels, handlers, and integration with the Configuration module.
ovadare/utils/logging_config.py

functions.name: ovadare.utils.logging_config.setup_logging
documentation: Sets up logging configuration based on settings from the Configuration module.

modules.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager
documentation:
Escalation Manager Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the EscalationManager class, which handles the escalation
of unresolved conflicts by notifying administrators via email and SMS. It
integrates with external services like SendGrid and Twilio, using the
SecretsManager to securely manage API keys and credentials.
ovadare/escalation/escalation_manager.py

classes.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager.EscalationManager

methods.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager.EscalationManager.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the EscalationManager.

Args:
    secrets_manager (SecretsManager): Manages secure access to API keys and credentials.

methods.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager.EscalationManager.escalate_conflict
documentation:
Escalates an unresolved conflict by notifying administrators.

Args:
    conflict (Conflict): The conflict to escalate.

methods.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager.EscalationManager._send_email
documentation:
Sends an email notification to administrators.

Args:
    subject (str): The email subject.
    body (str): The email body.

methods.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager.EscalationManager._send_sms
documentation:
Sends an SMS notification to administrators.

Args:
    message (str): The SMS message content.

methods.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager.EscalationManager._get_admin_emails
documentation:
Retrieves the list of administrator email addresses.

Returns:
    List[str]: A list of admin email addresses.

methods.name: ovadare.escalation.escalation_manager.EscalationManager._get_admin_phone_numbers
documentation:
Retrieves the list of administrator phone numbers.

Returns:
    List[str]: A list of admin phone numbers.
Handles escalation of unresolved conflicts by notifying administrators.

modules.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector
documentation:
Conflict Detector Module for the Ovadare Framework

This module provides the ConflictDetector class, which is responsible for detecting
policy violations by evaluating agent actions against the loaded policies.
It stores detected conflicts and provides methods for retrieving and resolving them.
ovadare/conflicts/conflict_detector.py

functions.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.load_conflicts
documentation: Loads conflicts from persistent storage.

classes.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the ConflictDetector.

Args:
    policy_manager (Optional[PolicyManager]): An instance of PolicyManager.
        If None, a new PolicyManager is created.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector.detect
documentation:
Detects conflicts by evaluating an agent's action against policies.

Args:
    agent_id (str): The ID of the agent performing the action.
    action (Dict[str, Any]): The action to evaluate.

Returns:
    List[Conflict]: A list of detected conflicts.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector.get_conflict_by_id
documentation:
Retrieves a conflict by its ID.

Args:
    conflict_id (str): The unique identifier of the conflict.

Returns:
    Optional[Conflict]: The Conflict instance if found, else None.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector.get_all_conflicts
documentation:
Retrieves all detected conflicts.

Returns:
    List[Conflict]: A list of all detected conflicts.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector.resolve_conflict
documentation:
Resolves a conflict by removing it from the stored conflicts.

Args:
    conflict_id (str): The unique identifier of the conflict to resolve.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector.clear_conflicts
documentation: Clears all stored conflicts.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector._load_conflicts
documentation: Loads conflicts data from the storage file.

methods.name: ovadare.conflicts.conflict_detector.ConflictDetector._save_conflicts
documentation: Saves conflicts data to the storage file.
Evaluates agent actions against policies to detect conflicts.
